summary.intent: Replace polling-based URL tracking in the browser viewer with event-driven URL updates using CDP navigation events to reduce unnecessary get_url calls.
summary.outcome: Implemented CDP EventFrameNavigated listener in the screencast relay to broadcast URL changes only when navigation occurs; removed get_url polling and the associated urlPollSuppressUntil suppression logic from the JS frontend.

repo: The screencast relay is in crates/browser/src/screencast.rs and handles both frame broadcasting and now URL change events via CDP.
Browser actions (get_url, evaluate) are logged at INFO level in moltis_browser::manager, making polling visible in logs.
chromiumoxide CDP bindings expose EventFrameNavigated with a non-optional Frame field; Frame.parent_id is Option but frame itself is not Option.

code.finding: ScreencastFrame struct gained an optional url field, populated only on navigation events rather than per-frame to avoid overhead.
URL display now reads from WebSocket frame metadata instead of a polling interval; urlPollSuppressUntil variable and its usage in navigateSession were removed entirely.

workflow: CDP EventFrameNavigated is the correct event for URL change detection in chromiumoxide; checking the generated bindings with grep was necessary to confirm the exact struct shape.
Test fixtures referencing structs with new fields need updating when fields are added without defaults.

friction: EventFrameNavigated struct shape was not known upfront — initial edit assumed frame was Option<Frame> but it is Frame directly, requiring a compile-check-and-fix cycle.
A test with a ScreencastFrame literal broke due to the new url field, requiring a second fix pass.
JS lint flagged urlPollSuppressUntil as unused after the polling was removed, requiring cleanup of both the variable declaration and its reference in navigateSession.
open_items: Scroll info still uses a polling interval — could potentially be replaced with a MutationObserver or scroll event listener for consistency.
No deduplication guard on EventFrameNavigated for same-URL navigations (e.g. hash changes or reloads) — may broadcast redundant URL updates.
